2605.02608.title: Dependency Parsing Across the Resource Spectrum: Evaluating Architectures on High and Low-Resource Languages
2605.02608.paper_summary: Transformer-based models achieve state-of-the-art dependency parsing for high-resource languages, yet their advantage over simpler architectures in low-resource settings remains poorly understood. We evaluate four parsers -- the Biaffine LSTM, Stack-Pointer Network, AfroXLMR-large, and RemBERT -- across ten typologically diverse languages, with a focus on low-resource African languages. We find that the Biaffine LSTM consistently outperforms transformer models in low-resource regimes, with transformers recovering their advantage as training data increases. The crossover falls within a resource range typical of treebanks for under-resourced languages. Morphological complexity (measured via MATTR) emerges as a significant secondary predictor of transformers' relative disadvantage after controlling for corpus size. These results indicate that the Biaffine LSTM may be better suited for syntactic tool development in low-resource regimes until sufficient annotated data is available to leverage the representational capacity of pre-trained transformers.
2605.02608.paper_summary_zh: 基於Transformer的模型在高資源語言的依存解析中達到了最先進的水平，但它們在低資源環境中相對於更簡單架構的優勢仍然不甚明瞭。<lb/>我們評估了四種解析器——Biaffine LSTM、Stack-Pointer Network、AfroXLMR-large 和 RemBERT——在十種類型學上多樣的語言中，特別關注低資源的非洲語言。<lb/>我們發現，Biaffine LSTM 在低資源環境中始終優於Transformer模型，隨著訓練數據的增加，Transformer恢復了它們的優勢。<lb/>這一交叉點落在低資源語言的樹庫的典型資源範圍內。<lb/>在控制語料庫大小後，形態複雜性（通過 MATTR 測量）顯示出Transformer相對劣勢的重要次要預測因子。<lb/>這些結果表明，Biaffine LSTM 可能更適合在低資源環境中進行語法工具的開發，直到有足夠的標註數據可用，以利用預訓練Transformer的表徵能力。
2605.02608.author: Kevin Guan et.al.
2605.02608.authors: Kevin Guan, Happy Buzaaba, Christiane Fellbaum
